claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 3 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit! Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Let's begin with some basic information about your child.

**First question: How old is your child?**

This helps me determine which eligibility rules apply:
- Children **under 16** may automatically qualify
- Children **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children **20 or over** are not eligible for Child Benefit

Please tell me your child's age (or if you have multiple children, let's start with one and go through each of them).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Oh, hello! Thanks for helping me with this. I've actually got three children I'd like to ask about - Alex, Blake, and Charlie. 

Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 2. They all live at home with me here in Manchester. Should I tell you about each of them separately, or...?

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! Thanks for providing that information - I'm happy to help you with all three of your children: Alex (age 3), Blake (age 8), and Charlie (age 2).

Since all three children are under 16 years old, they all fall into the same initial category. I can assess them together as we go through the questions, which will be more efficient for you!

Let me continue with the next check for children under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Are any of your children working 24 hours or more per week?**

Given that Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 2, I'm assuming they're not in paid work - is that correct?

(This rule mainly applies to older teenagers, but I need to confirm this for the assessment.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, none of them are working. They're all too young for that - they're just at home with me!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you for confirming that! Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Are any of your children receiving benefits in their own right?**

This would include things like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit claimed in their own name.

Again, given their young ages (3, 8, and 2), I'm expecting the answer is no - but I need to confirm this for the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them receive any benefits in their own names. They're just little ones!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Are any of your children in an apprenticeship?**

This mainly applies to older teenagers, but I need to check. Given that Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2) are all very young, I assume none of them are in apprenticeships - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in apprenticeships. They're far too young for that!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Now let's move on to questions about you as the claimant. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now let's talk about your residency status:**

You mentioned you live in Manchester with your children. To qualify for Child Benefit, you need to meet UK residency requirements. 

**Do any of these apply to you?**

1. You normally live in the UK (as a UK citizen or with permanent residence)
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working/studying/have sufficient resources
4. You're a Crown servant living abroad

Since you're living in Manchester, I'm assuming you normally reside in the UK - is that correct? Are you a UK citizen or do you have the right to reside in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - I normally live in the UK. We're based here in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Are you responsible for Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

To be considered responsible, you need to meet at least one of these conditions:

1. **You live with the child** (which you've already mentioned - they all live at home with you)
2. You contribute to their upkeep (food, clothes, housing, etc.)
3. You pay maintenance under a court order or agreement

Since you mentioned all three children live at home with you in Manchester, it sounds like you meet the responsibility requirement. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - they all live with me and I look after them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Important question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, or Charlie?**

For example:
- Another parent or guardian
- A former partner
- Another family member

Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child at a time. If someone else is claiming, you would need them to agree to stop their claim before you could claim.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of your three children?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Are any of your children currently:**

1. **In hospital** (as an inpatient)
2. **In care** (looked after by the local authority)
3. **Fostered** (living with foster carers)
4. **Living normally at home** with you

You mentioned they all live at home with you in Manchester. Are all three children living with you normally, or are any of them in hospital, care, or a fostering arrangement?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're all living normally at home with me. None of them are in hospital, care, or fostered.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final question: Is the local council paying towards any of your children's accommodation or maintenance?**

This would be things like the council paying for their housing or care costs. 

Since your children live at home with you and you're looking after them, I'm assuming the council is NOT paying for their accommodation or maintenance - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's correct - the council isn't paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. I'm looking after them myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Wonderful! Let me proceed to the final outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Excellent News!

**You ARE eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie!**

### Why you're eligible:

✅ **Alex (age 3)**, **Blake (age 8)**, and **Charlie (age 2)** all qualify as they are under 16 years old  
✅ You meet UK residency requirements (living in Manchester)  
✅ You are responsible for all three children who live with you  
✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them  
✅ They're all living normally at home with you  
✅ The local council isn't paying for their accommodation or maintenance

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and each child
3. **Provide birth certificates** (or adoption certificates if applicable) for Alex, Blake, and Charlie

### Important to Know:

💷 **High Income Child Benefit Charge**: If you or your partner earn over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit through the tax system. You can still claim but choose not to receive payments if you prefer.

💰 **Payment schedule**: Child Benefit is usually paid every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

### Apply here:
🔗 https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Congratulations, and best wishes to you and your family!

reasonings: Alex (age 3) is eligible for Child Benefit. As a child under 16, Alex automatically qualifies as a 'qualifying young person'. Alex is not working 24+ hours per week, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in a disqualifying apprenticeship. The claimant meets UK residency requirements (normally lives in Manchester), is responsible for Alex (lives with and looks after them), no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Alex is living normally at home, and the local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

Blake (age 8) is eligible for Child Benefit. As a child under 16, Blake automatically qualifies as a 'qualifying young person'. Blake is not working 24+ hours per week, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in a disqualifying apprenticeship. The claimant meets UK residency requirements (normally lives in Manchester), is responsible for Blake (lives with and looks after them), no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, Blake is living normally at home, and the local council is not paying towards Blake's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

Charlie (age 2) is eligible for Child Benefit. As a child under 16, Charlie automatically qualifies as a 'qualifying young person'. Charlie is not working 24+ hours per week, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in a disqualifying apprenticeship. The claimant meets UK residency requirements (normally lives in Manchester), is responsible for Charlie (lives with and looks after them), no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, Charlie is living normally at home, and the local council is not paying towards Charlie's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for all three children (Alex age 3, Blake age 8, and Charlie age 2). All three children are under 16 and therefore automatically qualify as 'qualifying young persons'. The claimant meets all necessary conditions: they normally reside in the UK (Manchester), they are responsible for all three children who live with them at home, no other person is claiming Child Benefit for any of the children, all children are living normally at home (not in hospital, care, or fostering), and the local council is not paying towards any child's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility requirements under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)
